---
title: "get_top_titles_summary"
---

[< Back to Reference](../reference.qmd)

# `get_top_titles_summary()`

Summarize Top Titles Over a Date Range

## Usage

```r
get_top_titles_summary(
  platform_name = "netflix",
  country_name = "United States",
  start_date,
  end_date,
  flix_type = "movies",
  n = 20,
  token = "FLIX_PATROL"
)
```

## Arguments

| Argument | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `platform_name` | Character. Platform name. |
| `country_name` | Character. Country name. |
| `start_date` | Date/Character. Start of the range. |
| `end_date` | Date/Character. End of the range. |
| `flix_type` | Character. Content type (default '"movies"'). |
| `n` | Integer. Number of top titles to return (default 20). |
| `token` | Character. API environment variable name. |

## Value

A tibble with columns 'title', 'days_on_chart', 'best_rank',
'avg_rank', 'first_seen', 'last_seen'.

## Description

Aggregates daily Top 10 data over a date range and returns a summary of
which titles appeared most frequently, along with their best rank and
average rank.
